title: Xylophacos casei
intro: Xylophacos casei, also known as Cape Mallow, is a perennial succulent plant native to South Africa, characterized by its striking flowers and fleshy leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, digestive problems, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Xylophacos casei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Cape Mallow is often prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is also used in topical creams and ointments to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing.
